summary: Quick Action Buttons testing completed. Dispatch Page shows large orange 'Dispatch' button (96x56px), Orders Page shows large green 'Receive' button (96x56px). Both buttons are touch-friendly and optimized for mobile. UI tests passed. Backend dispatch API works correctly. Critical bug found: Quick Receive modal sends order_item_id instead of dispatch_event_id to receive API, causing 422 errors.

integration_issues.issue: OrdersPage.jsx confirmReceive() sends order_item_id instead of dispatch_event_id. Backend /api/receive expects dispatch_event_id from DispatchEvent, not order item id. Causes 422 error on confirm receive.
integration_issues.fix_required: Need to first call /api/pending-receive to get dispatch_event_id for each order item, then use those IDs when calling receiveItem API

passed_tests: TEST 1: Dispatch Page - Quick Dispatch button visible on dispatch items
TEST 2: Dispatch Page - Quick Dispatch button is large (h-14=56px, w-24=96px) and touch-friendly
TEST 3: Dispatch Page - Quick Dispatch button has orange background (bg-orange-500)
TEST 4: Dispatch Page - Clicking Quick Dispatch opens dispatch modal immediately
TEST 5: Dispatch Page - Modal shows item details and quantity stats (Requested, Dispatched, Pending)
TEST 6: Dispatch Page - Quantity input and Notes field work correctly
TEST 7: Dispatch Page - Confirm dispatch works and updates queue
TEST 8: Orders Page - Quick Receive button visible for FULLY_DISPATCHED orders
TEST 9: Orders Page - Quick Receive button is large (h-14=56px, w-24=96px) and touch-friendly
TEST 10: Orders Page - Quick Receive button has green background (bg-green-500)
TEST 11: Orders Page - Clicking Quick Receive opens receive confirmation modal
TEST 12: Orders Page - Modal shows order info and items to receive with quantities
MOBILE TEST 2: Dispatch button opens modal on mobile tap
MOBILE TEST 4: Receive button opens modal on mobile tap
failed_tests: TEST 13: Orders Page - Confirm receive fails with 422 error (order_item_id vs dispatch_event_id mismatch)

passed: GET /api/dispatch-queue - Returns dispatch items for admin
POST /api/receive with dispatch_event_id - Receives items correctly
POST /api/receive validates dispatch_event_id exists (returns 404 for invalid)

action_items: FIX (HIGH): OrdersPage.jsx confirmReceive() - Need to get dispatch_event_id from /api/pending-receive before calling receiveItem. Current code uses item.id (order_item_id) but backend expects dispatch_event_id
The receive flow should: 1) Call pending-receive API 2) Match dispatch events to order items 3) Call receive API with dispatch_event_id for each event
critical_code_review_comments: OrdersPage.jsx line 93-98: receiveItem is called with {order_item_id: item.id} but backend ReceiveCreate schema expects {dispatch_event_id: int}. This is the root cause of 422 error.
seed_data_creation: Created test orders for dispatch queue testing
context_for_next_testing_agent: Quick Action buttons UI is complete and touch-friendly. Quick Dispatch works end-to-end. Quick Receive has integration bug - frontend sends order_item_id but backend expects dispatch_event_id. Test order TEST-DISPATCH-101 has FULLY_DISPATCHED status for testing receive flow once fixed.

rca_of_the_issue.root_cause: API contract mismatch between frontend and backend. Frontend OrdersPage.jsx calls receiveItem({order_item_id: item.id, quantity_received: ...}) but backend ReceiveCreate schema expects {dispatch_event_id: int, quantity_received: int}
rca_of_the_issue.mitigation: Update OrdersPage.jsx confirmReceive() to: 1) First fetch dispatch events via getPendingReceive() 2) Filter events for the selected order 3) Call receiveItem with dispatch_event_id from each event
